updates.summary: This is a low-risk patch release containing three bug fixes for ESLint rule autofixers and suggestions. The fixes address correctness issues in `prefer-optional-chain`, `no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare`, and `no-unnecessary-type-assertion` rules, primarily improving fixer safety and accuracy. No breaking changes are introduced.
ramifications: The `prefer-optional-chain` rule now uses a suggestion instead of an autofix for trailing binary operators, meaning previously auto-applied fixes for this case will now require manual acceptance.

The `no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare` fixer now correctly preserves boolean results for nullable true comparisons, which could change the output of previously auto-fixed code if the rule was applied.
The `no-unnecessary-type-assertion` fixer now properly parenthesizes object literals at the left edge of expression statements, preventing potentially invalid syntax in auto-fixed code.
If your codebase relies on automated lint fixing in CI, the behavior of these three rules may subtly change in edge cases, warranting a review of any affected files.

updates.summary: This is a low-risk patch release focused entirely on bug fixes in the eslint-plugin, not the parser itself. Since these are correction fixes to existing linting rules rather than parser changes, upgrading should be safe with minimal impact.
ramifications: The prefer-optional-chain rule now uses a suggestion instead of an autofix for trailing binary operators, meaning auto-fix tools will no longer automatically apply this change — developers must manually accept the suggestion.
The no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare fixer now correctly preserves boolean results for nullable true comparisons, which may change the output of previously auto-fixed code.
The no-unnecessary-type-assertion fixer now parenthesizes object literals at the left edge of expression statements, preventing potential syntax errors in auto-fixed code.
If your CI pipeline enforces lint autofixing with --fix, the prefer-optional-chain behavior change could result in fewer automatic fixes being applied.
